prerequisites: An active Keka account with an API subscription. The Keka API is an add-on feature that requires a subscription for customer access.
Global admin access in Keka. Only the Global admin can generate and manage API keys.
A generated client ID and client secret, created under Global admin settings -> Integrations & Automations -> API access.
An API key generated with the required privileges and scopes for the data you intend to read.
Your Keka environment base URL (production login.keka.com, or the kekademo.com sandbox) to target the correct identity/token endpoint.

authMethods.name: API key with client credentials (access token)
authMethods.summary: Keka authenticates by exchanging credentials for an access token. POST grant_type, scope, client_id, client_secret, and api_key as form-url-encoded fields to the Keka identity token endpoint (https://login.keka.com/connect/token in production, https://login.kekademo.com/connect/token in sandbox) and use the returned token to call the API. In this connector the client_secret is the stored secret field; client_id, api_key, grant_type, and scope are supplied as configuration. The grant_type and scope are both fixed to the value kekaapi.

setupSteps.title: Confirm API access on your Keka subscription
setupSteps.body: Verify your Keka plan includes API access. Keka exposes the API as an add-on feature that requires a subscription, so confirm it is enabled before generating credentials.

setupSteps.title: Generate the client ID and client secret
setupSteps.body: Sign in as a Global admin and go to Global admin settings -> Integrations & Automations -> API access. Generate the client ID and client secret. Only the Global admin can create and manage these credentials.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key with privileges and scopes
setupSteps.body: In the same API access section, create a new API key by selecting the required privileges and scopes for the data you want to read. API keys have no default expiry; optionally enable Set expiry to configure one. Record the api_key, client_id, and client_secret values.

setupSteps.title: Enter the credentials and fixed token parameters
setupSteps.body: Provide api_key, client_id, and client_secret in the connector. Set grant_type to kekaapi and scope to kekaapi (both are the fixed required values for the token request). The client_secret is stored as the connector secret; the connector exchanges these for an access token at the Keka token endpoint to call the API.

sources.title: Generate Access Token using API Key
